conversations.say: Inez, outsiders are poking around the counter records. We need to agree on what neighborhood details stay off-limits before financial and investigative pressure turns every irregularity into guilt.
conversations.offer: To consult with you on neighborhood privacy boundaries before showing any part of the ledger.
conversations.request: Your support and protection for the shop's informal arrangements if the ledger alteration surfaces.

conversations.say: Morgan, you want to date the pattern change in the records, but my customers aren't content for your investigation. We need clear terms on attribution and customer privacy first.
conversations.offer: A bounded discussion of the transaction pattern and continuity details.
conversations.request: A firm promise not to call any customer R or expose neighborhood names.

conversations.say: Frankie, you love a stable human habit. Tell me how you remember R's supposed private habit before I show you what my ledger actually contains.
conversations.offer: To compare notes on the private habit observed across transactions.
conversations.request: Your exact recollection of R's routine without turning it into theatrical authentication.

public_statement: Fifteen years of keeping a counter means remembering who paid in loose coin and who left packages in the rain. Before anyone treats my ledger as an open book, remember that a shop protects the people who keep it alive.

commitments.promise: To protect local customer privacy and keep informal arrangements confidential until safeguards are established.
Not to disclose customer names or treat ordinary transactions as proof of R's identity without agreed attribution terms.

correction: The transaction records show continuity and several collectors, but do not prove a single human author or resident behind the account.
goal_progress: Initiated boundary discussion with Inez regarding neighborhood privacy.
Opened terms negotiation with Morgan regarding ledger attribution.
Engaged Frankie on the recurring private habit.
reasoning_summary: Rafi Calderón opens Section 1 by securing neighborhood alignment with Inez (C16), setting firm privacy terms with Morgan (C14) regarding ledger access, and comparing notes with Frankie (C02) on transaction habits. Beliefs favor a human explanation given personal familiarity with the routines, while remaining cautious about premature conclusions.
